nodes.name: Wazuh Alert Webhook
nodes.notes: Receives JSON from wazuh_thehive_bridge.py for alerts at/above N8N_MIN_LEVEL (default 10). See CLI-REFERENCE.md 'n8n SOAR setup' for how to wire the bridge's N8N_WEBHOOK_URL to this node's webhook URL.

nodes.name: Is Critical?
nodes.notes: Single-threshold routing: critical-tier alerts (Wazuh level >= 13) get an extra escalation log entry on top of the case TheHive gets either way. Swap the 'Log Escalation' node below for a Slack/Discord/Email node later without touching anything upstream.

nodes.name: Create TheHive Case
nodes.notes: Uses the 'TheHive API Key' header-auth credential (Authorization: Bearer <key>). See CLI-REFERENCE.md 'n8n SOAR setup' to create it. THEHIVE_URL env var defaults to the in-network service name; override in n8n's docker-compose environment if needed.

nodes.name: Assign Triage Queue
nodes.notes: Optional: set SOC_TRIAGE_ANALYST in n8n's environment (a TheHive username) to auto-assign every SOAR-created case to a default analyst/queue. Leave unset to skip assignment and leave cases unassigned for manual pickup.

nodes.name: Log Escalation (replace with notify)
nodes.notes: Placeholder notification step. To wire real alerting, delete this node and connect 'Assign Triage Queue' -> a Slack/Discord/Send Email node instead. Kept as a Code node so the workflow runs end-to-end with zero external notification credentials required.

meta.templateNote: Both outputs of 'Is Critical?' currently route to the same 'Create TheHive Case' node, so every alert that reaches this workflow (i.e. already >= N8N_MIN_LEVEL from the bridge) gets a case either way. The branch exists so you can easily split behavior later -- e.g. point the TRUE output (critical) at a faster/different notification path -- without restructuring the workflow. See CLI-REFERENCE.md 'n8n SOAR setup' for the single-threshold model this lab uses: the bridge's N8N_MIN_LEVEL controls whether an alert reaches n8n at all (the actual triage threshold); the 'Is Critical?' node inside n8n controls only the escalation-logging branch within that already-high-severity population.
